channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pro-Trump Arab American group changes its name after the president's Gaza 'Riviera' comments
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — A group that played a key role in Donald Trump’s voter outreach to the Arab American community alongside his allies is rebranding itself after the president said that the U.S. would “take over” the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 accused in Aurora kidnapping, torture appear in court
item.description: Three men accused of kidnapping and torturing a couple at an Aurora apartment complex were in court Monday.
item.dc:creator: Vicente Arenas, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship order is put on hold by a second federal judge
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered a second nationwide pause on President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally, calling citizenship a “most precious right.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine wants partner countries to join in postwar development worth billions
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine wants to collaborate with partner countries on postwar projects worth billions of dollars not just in mining rare earth elements, but also in energy and construction sectors to help rebuild the country, Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect arrested in fatal shooting at a cosmetics warehouse in Ohio, officials say
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A suspect in the shooting at an Ohio warehouse that killed one person and wounded five was arrested Wednesday morning in Columbus, authorities said. Chief Deputy U.S. Marshal Dan Deville said Bruce Reginald Foster III was located in an apartment and arrested around 10 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump will sign an executive order barring transgender female athletes from competing
item.description: President Donald Trump will sign an executive order on Wednesday designed to prevent people who were biologically assigned male at birth from participating in women’s or girls' sporting events.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk shocks lawmakers, setting himself on collision course
item.description: Billionaire businessman Elon Musk is on a collision course with lawmakers on Capitol Hill who are starting to challenge his authority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protests against Trump and Project 2025 are planned in cities across the US
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday. The movement has organized under the hashtags #buildtheresistance and #50501, which stands for 50 protests, 50 states, one day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas man set to be executed for killing a pastor in his own church
item.description: A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his own church during a robbery was facing execution on Wednesday, the second scheduled execution in the U.S. so far this year. Steven Lawayne Nelson was condemned for the 2011 killing of Rev.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio" on the same day that Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into, federal authorities said in court documents relea
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi to lead Justice Department
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday voted to confirm Pam Bondi as the next attorney general, giving her control of a Justice Department embroiled in controversy. The vote was 54-46. Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) joined all Republicans in voting in support of Bondi.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: 2 men facing charges following courtroom brawl in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – A routine court hearing devolved into chaos when two people in the gallery rushed at the defendant and ended up starting a brawl. Now, those two men are facing charges. It happened at a district court hearing Friday morning for murder suspect Alexander Ortiz.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, KRON4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Russian, Uzbek nationals filed 132 fake voter applications in Pinellas County: DOJ
item.description: The 132 applications were submitted in envelopes with "identically formatted return and address labels," the complaint said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: NTSB: Black Hawk was flying too high when it collided with passenger plane over Washington DC, killing 67
item.description: Data retrieved by the National Transportation Safety Board confirmed the Army Black Hawk helicopter that crashed into a passenger plane near D.C. was flying too high.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims of the midair collision near DC recovered as NTSB probes altitude data
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — The remains of all 67 victims of last week's midair collision of an American Airlines flight and an Army helicopter near the nation's capital have been recovered, authorities said Tuesday. All but one has been identified.
item.dc:creator: SERKAN GURBUZ and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 bodies recovered from tent in Fort Collins drainage tunnel
item.description: When officers arrived, they found the two people dead inside a tent that had been set up inside the tunnel. Police believe they had crawled under safety gates to get inside the tunnel.
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Donnell, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Designs unveiled for replacement of Baltimore bridge almost a year after deadly collapse
item.description: SPARROWS POINT, Md. (AP) — Almost a year after the deadly collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland officials on Tuesday unveiled their designs for its replacement, which will be taller and better protected against ship strikes.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former paraprofessional sentenced to 4 years for sexual assault of student
item.description: Imagine Ewer, 28, pleaded guilty to one charge of first-degree contributing to the delinquency of a minor and one charge of sexual assault on a child by one in a position of trust.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: What did US get from deals to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico? Not that much, observers say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico — now on hold for a month — risked blowing up North America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARÍA VERZA and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trayon White expelled from the DC Council for corruption but he's free to run for his old seat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The D.C. Council voted unanimously Tuesday to expel Councilmember Trayon White, who faces a federal trial on charges of taking tens of thousands of dollars in bribes to influence city contract decisions. White, 40, was arrested by the FBI last August.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden signs with powerful Hollywood agency
item.description: Is former President Joe Biden Hollywood's next leading man? The 46th president has signed with behemoth talent firm Creative Artists Agency to handle his professional life after leaving office, the agency announced.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans: Rubio
item.description: The U.S. government cannot deport American citizens and such a move would be met with significant legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: China counters with tariffs on US products. It will also investigate Google
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China countered President Donald Trump's across-the-board tariffs on Chinese products with tariffs on select U.S. imports Tuesday, as well as announcing an antitrust investigation into Google and other trade measures. U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and HUIZHONG WU Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday agreed to a 30-day pause on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada as America's two largest trading partners took steps to appease his concerns about border security and drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, ABC 4

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
